mnemonics: JToggleButton is like a light switch. It's either ON (selected) or OFF (unselected), unlike a JButton which is just a momentary press.
keyPoints: A JToggleButton is a two-state button (selected/unselected)
It is the superclass for other toggle components like JCheckBox and JRadioButton
Use ButtonGroup to group multiple JToggleButtons so that only one can be selected at a time
commonMistakes: Forgetting to add JToggleButtons to a ButtonGroup for mutual exclusivity (radio button behavior)
Confusing JToggleButton with JButton and not handling the selected state change event
